_doc: Implementation backlog for the single sequential Ralph loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH for done/not-done + dependency order; docs/HARNESS.md §8.1 = schema. Each task's `do` + `done-when` live in a per-task Markdown spec at the `spec` path (.harness/tasks/TNNN.md, sections '## Do' / '## Done when'); every BUILDABLE task carries `facets` (difficulty auto-tuning) — gated (gate / needs-human) tasks are carved out and carry neither.

tasks.title: Document the harness dashboard in README
Fade out the 'All done!' celebration with easeOut instead of easeIn
verify: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild build -project Basket.xcodeproj -scheme Basket -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro'

./build_run.sh, then in the running simulator add exactly one item, check it off, and watch the full 'All done!' celebration overlay play out: it should fade in, hold, and then visibly fade its opacity down smoothly over the last portion of its exit instead of staying near-full-opacity and then snapping/clipping to gone

Re-check the file diff shows only the curve/duration constant changed on the exit withAnimation call in celebrateCleared(), not the 1.6s hold delay, the entry animation, or ClearedCelebration.swift's internal appear animation

tasks.title: Restrict Got-it row unmark to the check circle only
verify: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild build -project Basket.xcodeproj -scheme Basket -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro'

./build_run.sh, then in the running simulator: check an item off so it lands in the faded 'Got it' section, tap the row body away from the check circle (e.g. on the item name or empty space) and confirm the item stays checked/does not move back to the to-get list, then tap the check circle itself and confirm it does restore to the to-get list
Also tap-test a to-get row body still opens the quantity editor (showsQuantity path unchanged) and its own check circle still checks the item off

tasks.title: Dismiss the open quantity editor (and its keyboard) on an outside tap
verify: xcodegen generate && xcodebuild build -project Basket.xcodeproj -scheme Basket -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro'

./build_run.sh, then in the running simulator: tap a to-get row's '+ Qty' chip to expand its inline quantity editor, then tap blank space below the list (or any area not covered by a row/header/add bar) — the editor animates closed (expandedID clears) and no item's checked state changes
Re-expand the same row, tap the quantity number to raise the keyboard (numberPad or decimalPad), then tap blank space below the list — both the keyboard and the whole inline editor dismiss together

Confirm unaffected behavior still works: tapping a to-get row's body (or its chip) still opens/closes its own editor; tapping a DIFFERENT row's chip while one editor is open switches the expansion to the new row; the −/+ steppers, unit pills, and the X 'Clear quantity' button inside an open editor still work; tapping a 'Got it' row still restores it; the header title/info-button taps and the add bar still work unchanged

tasks.title: Fix vertical alignment of the tip-jar heart next to the "Basket" title
verify: xcodegen generate && ./build_run.sh reaches ** BUILD SUCCEEDED ** and launches on the simulator

Inspect the screenshot written to screenshots/ (or take a fresh one via build_run.sh) with tipJar.hasTipped forced true — confirm the heart.fill glyph now sits visually centered against the cap-height of the "Basket" title rather than sitting low/high relative to it
Toggle titleRainbow by tapping the title (per the existing onTapGesture) and confirm the heart's vertical position stays correct in both the rainbow and classic label states
